summary: Building ConnectSphere - a modern social media web app with React + TypeScript frontend and Motoko/Internet Computer backend. Features include user profiles, posts (text/image), likes/comments, stories, search, notifications, dark/light mode, and poll-based chat. Purple-blue gradient design with mobile-first responsive layout. UI should be distinctly different from Instagram's look and feel.
architectural_notes: Backend: Motoko canisters on Internet Computer (auth + database + storage)
Authentication: Internet Identity (supports Google, Apple, Microsoft)
Chat is poll-based (no WebSocket/real-time support on this platform)
Design: purple-blue gradient theme, rounded cards/buttons, smooth animations
UI must be visually distinct from Instagram — avoid similar layout patterns, icons, or interaction styles
known_issues: Short video uploads not supported - image posts only
Phone authentication not available via Internet Identity
Real-time chat uses polling instead of WebSockets

existing_features.title: Implement Internet Identity authentication so users can sign up and log in using passkeys or linked accounts (Google, Apple, Microsoft). On first login, prompt the user to complete a profile setup (username, display name, bio, avatar upload).

Create a user profile page that displays the user's avatar, display name, username, bio, follower count, and following count. Include buttons to follow/unfollow other users and to edit your own profile (update bio, avatar, display name).

Implement a post system allowing users to create posts with text content and optional image uploads, edit their own posts, and delete their own posts. Posts should appear in a scrollable home feed ordered by most recent.

Add like and comment functionality to posts. Each post card should display a like button with a count, and a comment button that opens a comment thread. Users should be able to add and delete their own comments.

Implement a Stories feature: users can post a story (image or short text) visible for 24 hours, after which it is automatically hidden. A Stories bar appears at the top of the home feed showing avatars of users with active stories.
Implement a Search page where users can search for other users by username or display name. Results should show avatar, display name, and username with a follow/unfollow button inline.

Build a Notifications page that lists activity events for the logged-in user: new followers, likes on their posts, and comments on their posts. Show the actor's avatar, a short description, and timestamp. Mark notifications as read when viewed.

Implement a poll-based chat/messaging system. Users can open a direct message thread with any other user, send text messages, and receive messages. The frontend should poll for new messages every few seconds to simulate near-real-time updates.
Add a Dark Mode / Light Mode toggle accessible from the profile or settings area. The selected mode should persist across sessions.

Implement a fixed bottom navigation bar with five tabs: Home (feed), Search, Add Post (opens post composer), Chat, and Profile. Each tab should have a recognizable icon and label. The active tab should be visually highlighted.

Apply a modern purple-blue gradient visual theme throughout the app. Use rounded cards and buttons, smooth page transition animations, and a mobile-first responsive layout optimized for narrow screens. Typography should be clean and modern. Enforce the theme consistently across all pages including dark mode variants.
Use the generated app logo and splash background as static assets rendered in the login/landing page and the app header.

Redesign the StoriesStrip component to replace circular avatar bubbles (Instagram-style) with a horizontal card strip using rectangular or pill-shaped story cards that display the user's name below a square/rounded-rectangle thumbnail with a distinct colored border indicator for unseen stories.

Redesign the PostCard component to move away from the Instagram-style card layout. Use a content-first layout: place the post text/image prominently at the top, move the author info (avatar, display name, username, timestamp) into a compact inline row below the content, and replace the three-dot overflow menu with an inline icon row for actions. The like and comment counts should be displayed as pill badges rather than plain text counters.

Redesign the ProfilePage to replace the Instagram-style 3-column post grid and top stats row. Use a masonry or 2-column asymmetric grid for posts, and display follower/following stats as large typographic counters in a horizontal band with labels beneath them rather than as small inline numbers. The edit profile button should be a full-width outlined button styled with the app's gradient theme.

Redesign the BottomNav to use a floating pill-shaped navigation bar instead of a full-width flat tab bar. The nav should float above the bottom edge with a shadow and backdrop blur effect. The active tab indicator should be a filled background highlight within the pill rather than an underline or icon color change. The Create Post button should be an elevated square icon button within the pill.

Redesign the HomePage feed layout so it does not resemble Instagram's feed. Use a card-based feed with visible card background panels (using the app's glass/gradient card style) and left-accented colored borders per post type (text vs. image). Add a visual divider between the stories strip and the feed. Remove any full-bleed edge-to-edge image layout that mimics Instagram.

Redesign the LandingPage hero section to use an angled split layout or asymmetric two-panel design rather than a centered hero. The left/top panel should show the app logo and tagline; the right/bottom panel should show a stylized feature showcase or gradient graphic element. Replace any generic rounded CTA button with a distinctive styled button using the app's gradient and a unique shape (e.g., chamfered corners or arrow suffix).

new_feature_requests.summary: User authentication via Internet Identity (Google, Apple, Microsoft)
User profile with avatar, bio, followers & following count
Bottom navigation bar (Home, Search, Add Post, Chat, Profile)
Redesign UI elements that look similar to Instagram to give ConnectSphere a distinct, original visual identity
